commands.review_summary:
The PR changes model-call diagnostics to count streamed response bytes from snapshotless stream events, updates OTEL/docs wording, and adds focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +22, Tests +104, Docs 0. Total +126 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main stringifies whole stream chunks while the stream protocol and providers carry accumulated `partial` snapshots on deltas. I did not run tests in this read-only review, but the PR adds focused regression coverage for the path.
commands.review_followup: - Maintainer review should explicitly accept the response-byte metric rebaseline and confirm the PR remains scoped to diagnostics rather than the full Windows starvation report.

target.title: fix(agents): count stream deltas incrementally
target.body:
## Summary

- fixes the diagnostics stream-byte counter introduced by `c6e98493511b3a18e6f2f402693ef7dc832a071f` so plain stream deltas are counted from the new event shape without accumulated `partial` snapshots
- avoids serializing full assistant snapshots on every streamed chunk, removing the repeated answer-so-far diagnostic work called out in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599#issuecomment-4555471713
- keeps diagnostic byte inspection best-effort so opaque or hostile chunk shapes cannot break the model stream
- documents that response byte metrics exclude accumulated `partial` snapshots on delta events

Refs https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599.

## Verification

Behavior addressed: model-call diagnostics no longer repeatedly stringify full accumulated assistant partial snapshots for ordinary `text_delta`, `thinking_delta`, and `toolcall_delta` stream chunks.

Real environment tested: focused local Vitest wrapper, formatting/lint checks, branch autoreview, and AWS Crabbox Linux changed gate. Blacksmith Testbox proof is still blocked locally by missing Blacksmith auth, so the broad remote gate used direct AWS Crabbox.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts --reporter=dot
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.ts docs/logging.md docs/gateway/opentelemetry.md
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.ts
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main
node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --target linux --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 180m --timing-json --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"
```

Evidence after fix: final refreshed head `51c28e374fbb`; focused local wrapper passed `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts` with 13/13 tests after rebasing onto current `origin/main`; oxfmt, oxlint, and diff checks passed; docs and OTEL metric wording now define the snapshotless response-byte contract; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings with `overall: patch is correct (0.85)`; AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` passed with provider `aws`, lease `cbx_1e390d706444`, slug `tidal-lobster`, run `run_63b3d0b5c018`, machine `c7a.8xlarge`, exit `0`, `leaseStopped=true`. The changed gate selected lanes `core`, `coreTests`, `extensions`, `extensionTests`, and `docs`.

Observed result after fix: regression coverage proves diagnostic byte accounting does not call `toJSON` on full partial snapshots for text deltas, counts only incremental delta bytes, and keeps streaming alive when byte inspection cannot inspect a chunk.

What was not tested: Windows local-daemon reproduction from https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599 was not rerun in this PR. This removes the diagnostics amplification from the starvation cluster but does not claim to close every related local-provider responsiveness path alone.
target.author: vincentkoc
